name: Lentil Savoy
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the lentils and cook in 1 1/2 cups of vegetable stock. Use a rice cooker for this step.
2. While the lentils are cooking, cut the stems off the kale. Chop stems into half-inch pieces.
3. Chop the red bell pepper.
4. Put the pepper and stems in a large pan with enough stock to cover the bottom (about 1/4 inch). Cook on medium for a couple of minutes, then turn down the flame.
5. Wash and chop mushrooms. Add them to the pan.
6. Around 20-30 minutes after starting step 1 (around 5-10 minutes after finishing step 5), add the lentils to the pan. If using a rice cooker, manually shut it off without waiting for it to finish automatically.
7. Add cinnamon and other spices as desired. Stir the mixture in the pan, then add the kale leaves on top. Do not mix them in.
8. Cook for another 15 minutes or so until the leaves have a buttery taste.
9. Remove the kale from the top and serve separately.
Enjoy with black pepper available at the table. For an extra flavor boost, try sprinkling nutritional yeast over the lentil stew.
